title: (No AI) Read OpenAI’s latest internal memo about beating the competition — including Anthropic
author: Hayden Field
content:
![An illustration showing a computer with the OpenAI logo.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/04/STK_414_AI_CHATBOT_R2_CVirginia_B.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

OpenAI released a report breaking down how people use ChatGPT and who they are. | Image: The Verge

OpenAI's chief revenue officer, Denise Dresser, sent a four-page memo to employees on Sunday about the company's strategic direction, emphasizing the need to lock in users and grow its enterprise business.

The memo, which was viewed by _The Verge_, repeatedly underlines the importance of building a moat around its AI products, to combat how easy it is for users to switch between whichever model is topping the charts on any given day or week. Dresser, who [recently](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/906965/openais-agi-boss-is-taking-a-leave-of-absence) took over much of former COO Brad Lightcap's duties as he transitions to a new role focused on special projects, also emphasizes the importance of focusing on enterprise clients. It …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/911118/openai-memo-cro-ai-competition-anthropic)
contentSnippet:

The memo, which was viewed by The Verge, repeatedly underlines the importance of building a moat around its AI products, to combat how easy it is for users to switch between whichever model is topping the charts on any given day or week. Dresser, who recently took over much of former COO Brad Lightcap's duties as he transitions to a new role focused on special projects, also emphasizes the importance of focusing on enterprise clients. It …

title: (No AI) Microsoft is testing OpenClaw-like AI bots for 365 Copilot
author: Emma Roth
content:
![The OpenClaw logo on a red background.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/02/STKB382_OPEN_CLAW_C.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Microsoft is looking into ways it can integrate OpenClaw-style features into 365 Copilot, according to [a report from _The Information_](https://www.theinformation.com/articles/microsoft-plots-new-copilot-features-inspired-openclaw). The test reportedly comes as part of efforts to make its 365 Copilot AI assistant "run autonomously around the clock" while completing tasks on behalf of users.

Omar Shahine, Microsoft's corporate vice president, confirmed to _The Information_ that the company is "exploring the potential of technologies like OpenClaw in an enterprise context." OpenClaw is an open-source platform that allows users to create AI-powered agents that run locally on a user's device. The platform [rose in popularity](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/890517/openclaw-clawcon-meetup-nyc-open-source-ai) earlier this year, …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/911080/microsoft-ai-openclaw-365-businesses)

OpenClaw is an open-source platform that allows users to create AI-powered agents that run locally on a user's device. The platform rose in popularity earlier this year, …

creator: Dell Cameron
title: (No AI) Meta Is Warned That Facial Recognition Glasses Will Arm Sexual Predators
dc:creator: Dell Cameron
content: More than 70 organizations, including the ACLU, EPIC, and Fight for the Future, say the AI smart glasses feature would endanger abuse victims, immigrants, and LGBTQ+ people.
contentSnippet: More than 70 organizations, including the ACLU, EPIC, and Fight for the Future, say the AI smart glasses feature would endanger abuse victims, immigrants, and LGBTQ+ people.

creator: Kate Knibbs
title: (No AI) The Internet's Most Powerful Archiving Tool Is in Peril
dc:creator: Kate Knibbs
content: As major news outlets cut off the Wayback Machine, journalists and advocacy groups are rallying to protect the Internet Archive’s vast collection of web pages.
contentSnippet: As major news outlets cut off the Wayback Machine, journalists and advocacy groups are rallying to protect the Internet Archive’s vast collection of web pages.
